title: e2e: quadlet uses PODMAN env for podman binary path
body:
Adapts to pass the test even if
podman binary path is not `/usr/local/bin/podman`.

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

<!--

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening

comments.body:
This is not working, the CI and local test run use just bin/podman from the repository root.

See here how the podman path for quadlet is set: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/e7b9ae4f3bf1514e7f4f311c3fa3e083578db556/pkg/systemd/quadlet/podmancmdline.go#L11-L21

So we must set the `PODMAN` env var in the e2e test for a stable podman path. And only then we can match it.

Because you send a bunch of fixes for the e2e tests, which is very welcome btw, I let you know that I am working to migrate them to ginkgo v2: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/18163

This uncovered a lot of test issues that I am fixing there. Maybe it will uncover more issues in your environment as well.
